data: Iscrizioni greche lapidarie del Museo di Palermo
M.T. Manni Piraino, Iscrizioni greche lapidarie del Museo di Palermo, Sikelika 6 (Palermo: S. F. Flaccovio, 1973)
Described as a block of the local stone ('arenaria') from Monte Rosso by Mingazzini. the block appears largly intact, but with a break across the face
Boustrophedon across the upper part of the face, with a dividing line/ledge between the two lines.

End of 6th century BCE, beginning
                                5th century BCE
Found during excavations in the 1930s by Mingazzini, in re-use in 'Tower A' of a section of fourth-century BCE fortification, along with a second inscription.

Dedicated entirely to finds from the site of Monte Saraceno on the outside of town. Begins with a very small selection of Castelluccio pottery and then moves directly to 7th-6th century BC material. Spread over two large rooms, highlights include a satyr-donkey vase, an Attic vase with a sacrifice scene, an Otre in the shape of a tortoise amp; two Archaic Greek inscriptions. A splendid collection
Inscriptions grecques dialectales de Sicile : contribution à l'étude du vocabulaire grec colonial
